§6Level Emitters§f:
The §6ME Level Emitter §f(appliedenergistics2:part:280) is a §ecable part§f that emits a §bredstone signal§f based on the §bcount of a specific item§f in the network. It is the primary §eredstone output §f of an ME network and a building block for §6stock-keeping automation§f.

§6Production Path (network/parts/level_emitter.json)§f:
Shapeless recipe with 2 ingredients:
- 1× §6Redstone Torch§f (minecraft:redstone_torch)
- 1× §6Calculation Processor§f (appliedenergistics2:material:23)
- Yields 1× ME Level Emitter

The §6ME Fluid Level Emitter §f(appliedenergistics2:part:281) has the same recipe, substituting the Calculation Processor for a §6fluid-capable §fvariant — see the source recipe for the exact ore_dict.

§6Function (PartLevelEmitter.java:74-217)§f:
- §bConfigurable threshold§f: place an item in the §6config slot§f; the emitter reports its count
- §bRedstone output§f: emits §b15§f redstone strength when the count meets the threshold; §b0§f otherwise
- §bRedstone mode§f: §6HIGH_SIGNAL§f (default, emits high when §babove§f threshold) or §6LOW_SIGNAL §f(emits high when §bbelow§f threshold)
- §bFuzzy mode§f: §6IGNORE_ALL§f (default, exact match), §6PERCENT_50§f, §6PERCENT_99§f — for ore_dict equivalencies
- §bCrafting trigger§f: with a §6Crafting Upgrade§f, the emitter §brequests a crafting job§f when the count drops below threshold

§6Direct Descendants (TaskRetrieval graph)§f:
The jei-graph shows 5 items the Level Emitter is a component of:
- §6ME Storage Monitor§f (appliedenergistics2:part:400)
- §6ME Fluid Level Emitter §f(variant)
- §6Crafting patterns §fthat depend on level checks
- §6ME Inverted Toggle Bus §f(configuration)
- §6Redstone interface logic §f(pulses, gates)

§6Settings (PartLevelEmitter.java:114-117)§f:
- §6Settings.REDSTONE_EMITTER§f: §6HIGH_SIGNAL§f or §6LOW_SIGNAL §f (default: HIGH_SIGNAL)
- §6Settings.FUZZY_MODE§f: §6IGNORE_ALL§f, §6PERCENT_50§f, §6PERCENT_99 §f (default: IGNORE_ALL)
- §6Settings.LEVEL_TYPE§f: §6ITEM_LEVEL§f (default) or §6ENERGY_LEVEL§f
- §6Settings.CRAFT_VIA_REDSTONE§f: §6YES§f or §6NO§f — if YES, a redstone pulse triggers an autocraft

§6Practical Use§f:
- §eTip§f: pair a §6Level Emitter §f with a §6Crafting CPU §f and a §6Pattern Terminal §f to autostart jobs on demand
- §eTip§f: use §6LOW_SIGNAL §fmode with an §6Inverter Card §f to create a §6request-a-refill§f redstone signal
- §cWarning§f: a Level Emitter does not check §6NBT data §f unless the item is placed in the config slot with the §bexact same NBT§f; use a §6ME Storage Bus §ffor NBT-aware storage
- §cWarning§f: setting §6ENERGY_LEVEL§f without a §6Controller §f will show §b0 §f at all times
